text: Lysolecithins are amphipathic molecules with a hydrophilic head and hydrophobic tail that share many similar properties to surfactants. Due to the structural similarity to membrane lipids, surfactants are able to incorporate themselves into cell membranes and reduce the surface tension leading to a disruption of the stability of the membrane (Parsi, 2015). This detergent effect is theorized to also be able to occur with LPC considering their shared physicochemical properties, and therefore has been a point of investigation on the mechanisms of LPC-induced nervous cell death.
